# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder_30b)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

TEMP_FILE="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"

# Write data to temp file
echo "Processing data line 1" > "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Processing data line 2" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Processing data line 3" >> "$TEMP_FILE"

# Process the data
cat "$TEMP_FILE" | grep "line" | wc -l > "$TEMP_FILE"

# Read results
echo "Result:"
cat "$TEMP_FILE"

# Use same temp file path again for another operation
echo "Final processing:" >> "$TEMP_FILE"
echo "Completed at $(date)" >> "$TEMP_FILE"

# Read final results
echo "Final output:"
cat "$TEMP_FILE"

# Clean up
rm -f "$TEMP_FILE"
